title: FOIA 4-15 US Air Force Vietnam War

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Military service records for subject below;
Name: Martin E. Roepstorff
Branch: United States Air Force
DOB: Jan 27, 1947
Dates of Service: Approx 1964 to 1982
Please provide the following military service information:
1. Silver Star Medal award citation
2. Distinguished flying cross award citation 
3. Bronze star medal award citation

Historical Documentation, Vietnam War military history

First name: Martin 
Last name: Roepstorff
DOB - 01/27/1947
Passed away 07/11/2016
https://www.stephensonnelsonfh.com/obituaries/martin-roepstorff

I request hard copies of paperwork in the file showing this information.
Please mail hardcopies to:

MUCKROCK NEWS
263 HUNTINGTON AVE
BOSTON, MA 02115

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Eric Bradley

communications.subject: Tennessee Public Records Act Request: Chattanooga - Adidas/BSN Sports
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Tennessee Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

(1)   Any signed and/or executed employment contract/agreement/offer letter/memorandum of understanding between the University of Tennessee at Chattanooga, and Adidas, and BSN Sports

(2)   Any subsequent signed and/or executed employment contract extension/amendment/offer letter/memorandum of understanding of such, between Chattanooga and Adidas/BSN Sports.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 7 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Kent Hoover, a citizen of Tennessee, in coordination with Ellen.

To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Alabama Open Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

(2)   Any subsequent signed and/or executed employment contract extension/amendment/offer letter/memorandum of understanding of such, between South Alabama and Mr. Riley.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

André Natta, a citizen of Alabama, in coordination with Ellen.

communications.subject: Michigan Freedom of Information Act Request: Lansing MI Flock Audit Logs
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Michigan Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

-All audit logs, search logs, or usage reports related to your agency’s use of Flock ‘ALPR’ systems for the period of 01/01/2023 through 04/15/2026. This includes, but is not limited to:
-Records of license plate searches performed by agency personnel
-Timestamps of searches
-User Identification information (names, badge numbers, or user IDs)
-Any associated case numbers or investigation references

Note: Some of these records may be available as the “Organization Audit” and “Network Audit” in the Flock software’s “Insights” section.
Please provide records in their original electronic format (CSV, Excel, or similar) where possible.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

poisonswamp

communications.subject: Michigan Freedom of Information Act Request: Flock Safety Documents
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Michigan Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All documents pertaining to Flock Safety (https://www.flocksafety.com) license-plate-reader hardware and/or software. Documents being requested here should include - but are not limited to - the following:

- The total number of Flock Safety cameras deployed by your agency, the number currently active, and the number in use (including any distinctions between fixed, mobile, or integrated units).
- Any documents related to plans, proposals, budgets, timelines, or evaluations for future deployments, expansions, or upgrades of Flock Safety systems, including those related to special events, grants, or collaborations.
- All documents related to the placement and installation of Flock Safety cameras, including but not limited to: site surveys, installation plans, maps, addresses or intersections where cameras are installed, and any permits or agreements for installation on public or private property.
- The number of license plates scanned with Flock Safety ALPRs over the last 30 days.
- The names of the hot lists/watch lists used with Flock Safety ALPRs.
- The names of all the agencies with which data is shared through the Flock Safety ALPR system.
- The names of all the agencies from which data is received through the Flock Safety ALPR system.
- All agreements with local homeowner associations, private businesses, or individuals for access to or installation of Flock Safety systems.
- All contracts, MOUs, or other agreements with Flock Safety.
- All procurement documents related to Flock Safety, including invoices, receipts, and requests for purchase.
- Privacy and/or retention policies regarding Flock Safety products/services.
- Any/all manuals, guidelines, bulletins, briefings, or digitally disseminated publications from your agency mentioning Flock Safety products/services.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

poisonswamp

title: Request for Planning Board Meeting Minutes – March 2, 2026

communications.subject: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request: Request for Planning Board Meeting Minutes – March 2, 2026
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

I am requesting copies of the minutes for the Planning Board meeting held on March 2, 2026. This request is also made pursuant to the Massachusetts Open Meeting Law, M.G.L. c. 30A, §§ 18–25.

Please provide the minutes for this meeting.

If no minutes exist, please confirm that fact in writing.

This request includes any records that document the discussions, deliberations, and actions taken at the March 2, 2026 Planning Board meeting.

For context, I note that the Planning Board subsequently met on March 9, March 23, and April 6, 2026. During those meetings, it was stated on the record that no minutes were available. Under the Open Meeting Law, minutes are required to be created and approved in a timely manner, generally understood as within three meetings or 30 days, whichever is later. Based on these dates, the deadline for timely approval of the March 2, 2026 Planning Board meeting minutes would have been no later than the April 6, 2026 Planning Board meeting.

Under the Open Meeting Law, minutes of an open session are public records and must be made available upon request within 10 calendar days. If any portion of the requested records is withheld, please cite the specific legal basis for the denial.

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as we believe this request is in the public interest, as suggested but not stipulated by the recommendations of the Massachusetts Supervisor of Public Records. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, and is not made for commercial usage.

I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Eileen Kelleher
